mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are non-positive on average over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 5.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy dominates equity valuation by raising discount rates and debt service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS above 5 percent) and contracting industrial production (INDPRO declining over three months) predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because monetary restriction simultaneously compresses earnings and valuation multiples.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between the interaction term and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal underperforms the single-series FEDFUNDS model by more than 50 percent in Sharpe terms.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as restrictive policy curtails consumer spending and corporate top-line growth.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered observations during identified tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal rising capacity utilization and operating leverage, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a three-month window signals tight labor markets and strong household income growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average following UNRATE declines of more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal durable goods demand and construction sector strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST changes fail to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 52 percent of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms aggregate demand expansion and supports forward earnings estimates, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
